data.title: Scholastic falls after missing estimates, hit by education funding delays
data.description:
Scholastic (NASDAQ:SCHL) shares fell 13% in premarket trading on Friday after the company missed quarterly profit and revenue estimates amid a wider challenging environment.

Its education division faced sales pressure due to a volatile funding environment, including delayed and canceled federal education grants.

While ongoing tariff expenses were estimated at $10 million for the fiscal year, management indicated active monitoring of trade policy developments.

"Further, several states are facing budget impasses. In this challenging environment, we continue taking steps to strengthen this business for the long term," CEO Peter Warwick said. 

Scholastic (NASDAQ:SCHL) began fiscal 2026 with continued progress on cost discipline, IP-driven digital expansion, and real estate monetization initiatives, while affirming its full-year targets for revenue growth, profitability, and free cash flow. 

On guidance sensitivity, CFO Haji Glover highlighted potential retail market variability and tariff costs: "...the most important thing for us is the concerns of tariffs, as it reflects our business because we are a retail business. And those expenses, which we've already planned for, which is about $10 million this year."

Uncertainty in retail and school spending, as well as the pace of digital revenue recognition, were noted as potential variables impacting guidance.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, this company has a Hold rating with a score of 2.71 out of 5.

data.title: Nvidia plans $500 million investment in UK's self-driving startup Wayve
FDA nicotine pouch pilot to ease manufacturers' research burden - report
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration will ease requirements on nicotine pouch manufacturers under a new pilot program, Reuters reported on Friday, citing internal meeting transcripts.

The FDA looks to ensure products offer a net benefit to public health by helping smokers switch away from cigarettes without creating new issues, such as a surge in youth use.

Under the pilot, however, the FDA will drop a requirement that manufacturers submit product-specific studies in several areas, the report said. 

The pilot initiative directly impacts major tobacco companies Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) with its Zyn Ultra brand, Altria (NYSE:MO) with its on! and on! Plus brands, British American Tobacco (BTI) with its Velo mini brand, and Turning Point Brands (NYSE:TPB) with its Fre and Alp brands.

The FDA on Thursday  said that the pilot program it launched this month aims to increase efficiency and streamline the review process for premarket tobacco product applications (PMTAs) for nicotine pouch products.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: XXII, FDX, UPS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched up Friday morning, extending record-setting gains from the prior session as investors absorbed the Federal Reserve’s rate cut.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • 22nd Century Group (NASDAQ:XXII) +31% - Shares surged after the company announced it is now debt-free following repayment of $3.9M in senior secured debt. The move eliminates cash interest and amortization expense, lowering ongoing costs. Backed by new growth capital from its Series A preferred offering, XXII plans to expand its very low nicotine (VLN) tobacco leaf inventory, supporting production of 1M+ cartons of VLN products.
  • FedEx (NYSE:FDX) +5% - Shares climbed after the company beat FQ1 earnings estimates. For FY2026, FedEx projects 4%–6% revenue growth and EPS of $17.20–$19.00 (midpoint $18.10) vs. $18.36 consensus. “Our strategic initiatives, paired with our operational data platform moving 17M packages daily, position us to serve customers in any environment and drive long-term value,” said CEO Raj Subramaniam.
  • United Parcel Service (NYSE:UPS) +2% - Stock moved up, in tandem with FedEx's better-than-expected earnings report for the quarter, which lifted sentiment across the logistics sector. Separately, UPS confirmed it has scrapped its planned acquisition of Mexican courier Estafeta, stating that unmet closing conditions could not be resolved.

Biggest stock losers

  • Scholastic Corporation (NASDAQ:SCHL) -13% - Shares tumbled after missing FQ1 expectations, with Education Solutions revenue down 28% and Entertainment down 18%. Despite the miss, the company reaffirmed FY2026 guidance as adjusted EBITDA loss improved 8% to $55.7M, helped by cost cuts and stronger Children’s Publishing sales.
  • Lennar (NYSE:LEN) -3% - Shares of the home construction company slipped after Q3 results missed on both revenue and EPS. Net earnings fell ~50% Y/Y, with home sales revenue down 9% and gross margin contracting to 17.5% from 22.5% a year ago. For Q4, Lennar guided to 20K–21K new orders at an average sales price of $380K–$390K.

data.title: House Democrats call on FCC's Brendan Carr to resign over Kimmel suspension
data.description:
Top Democratic lawmakers have called for the resignation of Federal Communications Commission Chair Brendan Carr, following Disney (NYSE:DIS) unit ABC's decision to stop airing Jimmy Kimmel Live! indefinitely.

The late-night show was pulled off the air due to backlash over its host Kimmel's controversial comments about the killing of right-wing political activist Charlie Kirk.

The show's suspension came after Carr said on a podcast that ABC-affiliated stations "can find ways to change conduct and take action on Kimmel, or there's going to be additional work for the FCC ahead."

Carr "engaged in the corrupt abuse of power," House Democratic leaders said in a joint statement calling for his resignation. "He has disgraced the office he holds by bullying ABC, the employer of Jimmy Kimmel, and forcing the company to bend the knee to the Trump administration."

The statement came from House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-NY), Katherine Clark (D-MA), Pete Aguilar (D-CA), Ted Lieu (D-CA), Joe Neguse (D-CO), and Suzan DelBene (D-WA).

President Donald Trump on Thursday said local broadcast networks give him "totally bad publicity" and "maybe their license should be taken away," adding that the decision would be up to Carr.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nvidia hires Enfabrica CEO and licenses AI startup's tech for over $900M: report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) spent more than $900M to hire Enfabrica CEO Rochan Sankar and other employees and to license the AI hardware startup's technology, CNBC reported.

Nvidia is paying cash and stock in the deal, and the transaction closed last week. Enfabrica's CEO Sankar has joined Nvidia, the report added, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

An Nvidia spokesperson declined to comment in an email to Seeking Alpha. Enfabrica did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Enfabrica, which was founded in 2019, says its technology can connect over 100,000 graphics processing units, or GPUs, together. It’s a solution which could help Nvidia offer integrated systems around its chips so clusters can effectively serve as a single computer, the report added.

Nvidia previously invested in Enfabrica as part of a $125M Series B round in 2023 which was led by Atreides Management. The company did not reveal its valuation at the time, but said that it was a fivefold increase from its Series A funding, the report noted.

Late last year, Enfabrica raised another $115M from investors including Spark Capital, Arm, Samsung and Cisco. As per PitchBook, the post-money valuation was around $600 million, the report added.

The deal reminds of similar talent and technology acquisitions by companies such as Meta Platforms (META) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google.

In June, Meta announced a $14.3B investment in AI firm Scale, acquiring a 49% stake and valuing the San Francisco-based company at over $29B. Scale CEO Alexandr Wang joining Meta to head its “superintelligence” team focused on artificial general intelligence. In July, Google announced an agreement to bring in Varun Mohan, co-founder and CEO of Windsurf, and other research and development employees in a $2.4B deal that also included licensing fees.

Last year, Google made a similar deal to bring in the founders of Character.AI. Microsoft (META) did a similar deal for Inflection, as did Amazon for Adept.

On Thursday, Nvidia and Intel (INTC) announced a blockbuster deal to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

data.title: Santos CEO shows confidence in company, says no plans to retire - report
China state trader reportedly seeks to halt some BHP iron ore amid dispute
data.description:
• China’s state-run iron ore trader has told the country’s steel mills to temporarily stop using a popular product from BHP Group (NYSE:BHP) after talks on long-term contracts faltered, Bloomberg reported on Friday.
  • China Mineral Resources Group Co., created by Beijing to boost pricing power in the global iron ore trade, urged mills to suspend purchases of BHP’s Jimblebar blend fines from next week, the report said.
  • The call was echoed by the China Iron & Steel Association, according to the sources.

data.title: Uber-backed Moove seeks fresh capital at $2B valuation - report
data.description:
Moove, an African vehicle-financing startup backed by Uber (NYSE:UBER), is seeking to raise over $300 million at a valuation above $2 billion, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The funding would support expansion of its autonomous vehicle business, the people said.

Founded in 2020 by Ladi Delano and Jide Odunsi, Moove now operates in more than a dozen markets, including the UAE, Mexico, and India. Revenues have surged from around $50 million in 2024 to nearly $400 million in annualized recurring revenue, with the company breaking even on an EBITDA basis in late 2024.

In July, Moove raised over $1 billion in debt to finance an autonomous-driving rollout with Alphabet’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) Waymo and expand in the U.S. The company managed to secure debt in excess of $1 billion, one of the people said.

data.title: Activist investor Biglari targets Cracker Barrel CEO in eighth proxy fight
data.description:
Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (NASDAQ:CBRL) is facing more heat following its logo fiasco, this time from an activist investor launching his eighth proxy battle against the restaurant chain.

Sardar Biglari, CEO of rival chain Steak 'n Shake, has urged Cracker Barrel's (NASDAQ:CBRL) shareholders to vote against the re-election of its CEO Julie Felss Masino and director Gilbert Dávila to the company's board.

In a proxy filing, Biglari called Masino "worse than mediocre" and said the rebranding effort "ranked among this century's worst brand blunders alongside Bud Light and Jaguar."

"We have shown time and again that the board and management have made highly dubious decisions at every turn, whether it was contending that the company should focus on existing stores instead of engaging in a costly expansion plan (which saw nearly 60% of West Coast units closed) or that the company should not diversify into the bar business with the purchase of Punch Bowl Social (an investment that lost shareholders $137M in eight months)," the filing read.

Biglari said he'd warned Cracker Barrel (CBRL) in November 2024 against rebranding, saying it would not work and could damage the brand further.

The investor and his holding companies currently own 654,141 Cracker Barrel (CBRL) shares, or ~2.9% stake.

"We believe that Biglari's unprecedented seven proxy solicitations against the company in the past 14 years have been for purely self-interested reasons, and that his own actions and poor performance at Steak 'n Shake and Western Sizzlin' remain cautionary tales," a Cracker Barrel (CBRL) spokesperson told Fortune.

Biglari first launched a proxy fight to secure board seats at Cracker Barrel (CBRL) in 2011. He also owns stakes in restaurant chains Jack in the Box (JACK) and El Pollo Loco (LOCO).
